% -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
% DETERMINISTIC DISTANCE-BASED SAMPLING MODEL (both metrics)
% Canonical grid: r = pi * d^2 / s^2, s = 175m
% Hoeffding bound: k = log(2r/delta) / (2*epsilon^2), p = min(1, k/r)
% Zero fitted parameters: epsilon and delta are user-chosen conventions.
% -----------------------------------------------------------------------------

% Default parameters

\newcommand{\hoeffdingEpsilonCloseness}{0.05}

\newcommand{\hoeffdingDelta}{0.1}
\newcommand{\gridSpacing}{175}
\newcommand{\targetRho}{0.95}

% Deterministic sampling at standard distances (epsilon=0.05, delta=0.1, s=175m)
\newcommand{\hoeffdingKFiveKm}{2169}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingKTenKm}{2446}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingKTwentyKm}{2724}

% Deterministic sampling probability at standard distances

\newcommand{\hoeffdingPTwoKm}{100.0}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingPFiveKm}{84.6}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingPTenKm}{23.8}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingPTwentyKm}{6.6}

% Deterministic speedup at standard distances

\newcommand{\hoeffdingSpeedupTenKm}{4.2}
\newcommand{\hoeffdingSpeedupTwentyKm}{15.1}
